tpau-cpp-kernal/Location.h.name: Location
tpau-cpp-kernal/Location.h.detailed_description:
Represents a location within a file, including the file name, start position, and end position.

Line and column numbers are 1-based, and `0` indicates that it is not specified.

start.name: start
start.brief_description: The position where the location starts.

end.name: end
end.brief_description: The position where the location ends.

extend.name: extend
extend.detailed_description: Extend the location to include another location. If the other location is not in the same file, it will be ignored.

extend.name: extend
extend.detailed_description: Extend the location by a number of columns.

parameters.name: amount
parameters.description: The number of columns to extend the location by.

is_one_line.name: is_one_line
is_one_line.detailed_description: Check if the location is within a single line.
is_one_line.return_description: `true` if the location is within a single line, `false` otherwise.

width.name: width
width.detailed_description:
Get the width of the location in columns.

If the location is not on a single line, or no start or end column is known, `0` is returned.
width.return_description: The width of the location in columns, or `0` if it is not well defined.

empty.name: empty
empty.detailed_description: Check if the location is empty (i.e. has no file).
empty.return_description: `true` if the location is empty, `false` otherwise.

operator==.name: operator==
operator==.detailed_description: Check if two locations are equal.
operator==.return_description: `true` if the locations are equal, `false` otherwise.

operator!=.name: operator!=
operator!=.detailed_description: Check if two locations are not equal.

operator<.name: operator<
operator<.detailed_description:
Check if this location is before another location.

Locations are ordered first by line number, then by column number. Locations in different files are not comparable.
operator<.return_description: `true` if this location is before the other location, `false` otherwise.

operator>.name: operator>
operator>.detailed_description:
Check if this location is after another location.

Locations are ordered first by line number, then by column number. Locations in different files are not comparable.
operator>.return_description: `true` if this location is after the other location, `false` otherwise.

to_string.name: to_string
to_string.detailed_description: Convert the location to a string representation of the start of the location.
to_string.return_description: A string representation of the start of the location.

validate.name: validate
validate.detailed_description: Check that end is not before start.

exceptions.Exception: If location is invalid.

constructors.name: Location
constructors.detailed_description: Create a location with only a file name.

constructors.name: Location
constructors.detailed_description: Create a location.

parameters.name: line_number
parameters.description: The line number, or `0` if the line number is not specified.

parameters.name: start_column
parameters.description: The starting column number, or `0` if the column number is not specified.

parameters.name: end_column
parameters.description: The ending column number, or `0` if the column number is not specified.

constructors.name: Location
constructors.detailed_description: Create a location with a file name, line number, and column range.

parameters.description: The starting line number, or `0` if the line number is not specified.

parameters.name: end_line_number
parameters.description: The ending line number, or `0` if the line number is not specified.

parameters.name: end_column
parameters.description: The ending column number, or `0` if the column number is not specified.

constructors.name: Location
constructors.detailed_description: Create a location that covers the range from the start of one location to the end of another location. If the two locations are in different files, the second location will be ignored.

tpau-cpp-kernal/Symbol.h.name: Symbol
tpau-cpp-kernal/Symbol.h.detailed_description:
A Symbol represents a string in a way that allows copying and comparison in constant time.

Even though it uses the global symbol table and C++ does not guarantee the order of initialization of global variables, it is safe to use the `Symbol` constructor in global initializers.

id.name: id
id.brief_description: Pointer to the interned representation by the symbol.

empty_string.name: empty_string
empty_string.brief_description: The canonical representation of the empty string, used for empty symbols.

global.name: global
global.detailed_description:
The global symbol table.

It is a pointer to an object so we can force initialization before we use it in constructing symbols.

operator=.name: operator=
operator=.detailed_description: Assign the symbol corresponding to a string.

parameters.description: The string to create the symbol from.

str.detailed_description: Get the string represented by the symbol.
str.return_description: The string represented by the symbol.

c_str.detailed_description: Get the C string represented by the symbol.
c_str.return_description: The C string represented by the symbol.

empty.return_description: `true` if the symbol is empty, `false` otherwise.

operator!=.return_description: `true` if the symbols are not equal, `false` otherwise.

operator<.return_description: `true` if this symbol comes before the other symbol, `false` otherwise.

operator<=.name: operator<=
operator<=.detailed_description: Check if this symbol comes before or is equal to another symbol in lexicographical order.
operator<=.return_description: `true` if this symbol comes before or is equal to the other symbol, `false` otherwise.

operator>.name: operator>
operator>.detailed_description: Check if this symbol comes after another symbol in lexicographical order.
operator>.return_description: `true` if this symbol comes after the other symbol, `false` otherwise.

operator>=.name: operator>=
operator>=.detailed_description: Check if this symbol comes after or is equal to another symbol in lexicographical order.
operator>=.return_description: `true` if this symbol comes after or is equal to the other symbol, `false` otherwise.

operator bool.detailed_description: Check if the symbol is valid (not empty).
operator bool.return_description: `true` if the symbol is valid, `false` otherwise.

Interpret value as a boolean.

Numbers are `true` if they are nonzero, strings are `true` if they are nonempty. Binary data and void values can't be interpreted as booleans.

string_value.name: string_value

Get string representation of the value.

Numbers are converted to their string representation, strings are returned as they are. Boolean, binary data and void values can't be interpreted as strings.

default_size.detailed_description:
Get the default size of the value.

For integer values, this is the smallest size that can hold the value, or the explicitly set default size if it is larger. For binary data, this is its size in bytes.
default_size.return_description: The default size of the value, if it has a default size.

Command.name: Command

Base class for command line programs.

It only produces the output files if no errors occurred during processing.

Process the command line arguments and options.

This method must be implemented by subclasses to perform the actual processing of the command.

Create the output files for the command.

This method must be implemented by subclasses to create the output files. It will only be called if no errors occurred during processing.

Location.name: Location
Location.detailed_description:
Represents a location within a file, including the file name, start position, and end position.

Line and column numbers are 1-based, and `0` indicates that it is not specified.

start.name: start
start.brief_description: The position where the location starts.

end.name: end
end.brief_description: The position where the location ends.

extend.name: extend
extend.detailed_description: Extend the location to include another location. If the other location is not in the same file, it will be ignored.

extend.detailed_description: Extend the location by a number of columns.

parameters.description: The number of columns to extend the location by.

is_one_line.return_description: `true` if the location is within a single line, `false` otherwise.

width.name: width
width.detailed_description:
Get the width of the location in columns.

If the location is not on a single line, or no start or end column is known, `0` is returned.
width.return_description: The width of the location in columns, or `0` if it is not well defined.

empty.name: empty
empty.detailed_description: Check if the location is empty (i.e. has no file).
empty.return_description: `true` if the location is empty, `false` otherwise.

operator==.name: operator==
operator==.detailed_description: Check if two locations are equal.
operator==.return_description: `true` if the locations are equal, `false` otherwise.

operator!=.name: operator!=
operator!=.detailed_description: Check if two locations are not equal.

operator<.name: operator<
operator<.detailed_description:
Check if this location is before another location.

Locations are ordered first by line number, then by column number. Locations in different files are not comparable.
operator<.return_description: `true` if this location is before the other location, `false` otherwise.

operator>.name: operator>
operator>.detailed_description:
Check if this location is after another location.

Locations are ordered first by line number, then by column number. Locations in different files are not comparable.
operator>.return_description: `true` if this location is after the other location, `false` otherwise.

to_string.name: to_string
to_string.detailed_description: Convert the location to a string representation of the start of the location.
to_string.return_description: A string representation of the start of the location.

validate.name: validate
validate.detailed_description: Check that end is not before start.

exceptions.Exception: If location is invalid.

Command.name: Command
Command.detailed_description:
Base class for command line programs.

It only produces the output files if no errors occurred during processing.

commandline.name: commandline
commandline.detailed_description: The command line parser for the command.

arguments.name: arguments
arguments.detailed_description: The parsed command line arguments and options.

process.name: process
process.detailed_description:
Process the command line arguments and options.

This method must be implemented by subclasses to perform the actual processing of the command.

create_output.name: create_output
create_output.detailed_description:
Create the output files for the command.

This method must be implemented by subclasses to create the output files. It will only be called if no errors occurred during processing.

minimum_arguments.name: minimum_arguments
minimum_arguments.detailed_description:
Get the minimum number of arguments required for the command.

This method can be overridden by subclasses.
minimum_arguments.return_description: The minimum number of arguments required for the command. The default is 0.

maximum_arguments.name: maximum_arguments
maximum_arguments.detailed_description:
Get the maximum number of arguments allowed for the command.

This method can be overridden by subclasses.
maximum_arguments.return_description: The maximum number of arguments allowed for the command. The default is the maximum value of size_t, representing unlimited.

Location.name: Location
Location.detailed_description:
Represents a location within a file, including the file name, start position, and end position.

Line and column numbers are 1-based, and `0` indicates that it is not specified.

start.name: start
start.brief_description: The position where the location starts.

end.name: end
end.brief_description: The position where the location ends.

extend.name: extend
extend.detailed_description: Extend the location to include another location. If the other location is not in the same file, it will be ignored.

extend.name: extend
extend.detailed_description: Extend the location by a number of columns.

parameters.name: amount
parameters.description: The number of columns to extend the location by.

is_one_line.name: is_one_line
is_one_line.detailed_description: Check if the location is within a single line.
is_one_line.return_description: `true` if the location is within a single line, `false` otherwise.

width.name: width
width.detailed_description:
Get the width of the location in columns.

If the location is not on a single line, or no start or end column is known, `0` is returned.
width.return_description: The width of the location in columns, or `0` if it is not well defined.

empty.name: empty
empty.detailed_description: Check if the location is empty (i.e. has no file).
empty.return_description: `true` if the location is empty, `false` otherwise.

operator==.name: operator==
operator==.detailed_description: Check if two locations are equal.
operator==.return_description: `true` if the locations are equal, `false` otherwise.

operator!=.name: operator!=
operator!=.detailed_description: Check if two locations are not equal.

operator<.name: operator<
operator<.detailed_description:
Check if this location is before another location.

Locations are ordered first by line number, then by column number. Locations in different files are not comparable.
operator<.return_description: `true` if this location is before the other location, `false` otherwise.

operator>.name: operator>
operator>.detailed_description:
Check if this location is after another location.

Locations are ordered first by line number, then by column number. Locations in different files are not comparable.
operator>.return_description: `true` if this location is after the other location, `false` otherwise.

to_string.name: to_string
to_string.detailed_description: Convert the location to a string representation of the start of the location.
to_string.return_description: A string representation of the start of the location.

validate.name: validate
validate.detailed_description: Check that end is not before start.

exceptions.Exception: If location is invalid.

Symbol.name: Symbol
Symbol.detailed_description:
A Symbol represents a string in a way that allows copying and comparison in constant time.

Even though it uses the global symbol table and C++ does not guarantee the order of initialization of global variables, it is safe to use the `Symbol` constructor in global initializers.

global.detailed_description:
The global symbol table.

It is a pointer to an object so we can force initialization before we use it in constructing symbols.

Base class for command line programs.

It only produces the output files if no errors occurred during processing.
